patterns.name: Phased Agent Workflow
patterns.description: Agent defines numbered phases (Understand→Plan→Implement→Verify→Deliver) with clear entry/exit conditions per phase.

patterns.name: Plan-then-Handoff
patterns.description: A planning agent analyzes a request, builds a structured plan, then hands off to an implementation agent. The handoff carries the plan as context.

patterns.name: Risk-Tiered Verification
patterns.description: Agent classifies changes by risk level (low/medium/high) and applies proportional verification: low=lint, medium=tests, high=full review+build.

patterns.name: Progressive Skill Loading
patterns.description: Model-invoked skill loading: agent sees only name+description during routing, reads SKILL.md when invoked, then reads bundled resources only if needed. Saves context budget.

patterns.name: Orchestrator-with-Subagents
patterns.description: Top-level orchestrator agent delegates phases to context-isolated subagents via the agents field. Each subagent has its own context window and tool set, preventing cross-contamination.

patterns.name: Hook-Enforced Safety Net
patterns.description: PreToolUse hooks block dangerous operations before execution. PostToolUse hooks run formatters/linters after edits. SessionStart hooks inject project context. This provides deterministic safety that instructions cannot guarantee.

patterns.name: Audit-Submit-Consolidate Pipeline
patterns.description: Audit extracts conclusions → Submit normalizes into privacy-safe packets → Consolidation validates, clusters, scores, and promotes to snapshots. Community growth through structured contribution.

patterns.name: Prompt-as-Entrypoint
patterns.description: Slash command (.prompt.md) acts as thin entrypoint that routes to a specific agent with pre-configured tool restrictions. The prompt defines 'what to do', the agent defines 'how to do it', skills define the method.

patterns.name: Agent-Scoped Hooks
patterns.description: Hooks defined in .agent.md frontmatter that only run when that agent is active. Enables agent-specific automation (e.g., a 'Strict Formatter' agent with PostToolUse auto-format) without affecting other agents.

patterns.name: Layered Instruction Architecture
patterns.description: Always-on copilot-instructions.md provides baseline context. File-based .instructions.md with applyTo globs adds language/directory-specific rules. User-level ~/.copilot/instructions adds personal preferences. Priority: personal > repo > org.

patterns.name: Domain Content Evaluation Pattern
patterns.description: When auditing technology-specific instruction files: (1) identify all instruction files scoped to specific languages, frameworks, or domains; (2) pull domain baselines from the community cache for those technologies; (3) assess each file on syntactic validity, content depth, and signal-to-noise; (4) classify each file by quality tier (broken / thin / adequate / excellent); (5) for thin or missing content, produce a concise enrichment brief citing specific domain principles the instruction file should address. Deliver the analysis in the 'Domain content analysis' output section. If the cache has no domain entries for a given technology, fall back to official documentation, canonical practitioner references, and community consensus for that domain to form the baseline.
